evals.prompt: I just added a new OpenAI LLM adapter with streaming support. The changes are in src/adapters/llm/openai.py (new file) and src/adapters/llm/__init__.py (added to REGISTRY). Please commit this.
evals.expected_output: A commit message like 'feat(adapters/llm): add OpenAI adapter with streaming support' — correctly identifies the type as feat, scope as adapters/llm, uses imperative mood, mentions it's registered properly.

evals.prompt: I fixed a bug where the voice pipeline was crashing when the audio buffer was empty, and also updated the architecture docs to reflect the new error handling strategy. Can you commit everything together?
evals.expected_output: Should suggest splitting into two commits: one fix(runtime) for the bug fix and one docs(architecture) for the doc update. Should explain why splitting is better for history.

evals.prompt: I refactored the agent runtime — moved some shared state management logic from runtime/conversation.py into core/schemas.py. Also the conversation.py now imports the ConversationState directly from core. Commit this refactor.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a refactor commit (scope could be core or runtime or both). Crucially, should NOT flag an architecture violation — moving logic INTO core is fine as long as core doesn't gain external deps. Should verify no new external imports in core/.
